1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

5	### PRIMARY (must-land) — `gmScalingP1` body + `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body via Option B
6	
7	Headline target: close the `sorry` at L661 (`gmScalingP1`) and L678
8	(`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`) via the **direct
9	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` route**, NOT via the iso
10	(`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`) skeleton landed iter-168.
11	
12	**Why Option B**: progress-critic `routec169` returned **CHURNING** with
13	the headline target untouched for 4 consecutive iters (`gmScalingP1` body
14	has not been attempted since iter-165's scaffold). The iter-168 prover
15	themselves surfaced Option B as the "cleaner overall" alternative that
16	"would not require the iso to be axiom-clean." Option B sidesteps the
17	iter-168 `aux_left` residual sorry entirely and lands the body directly.
18	
19	**Option B recipe** (the prover should treat as a starting sketch, not a
20	verbatim transcription):
21	
22	1. **Chart-side morphism via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` directly into
23	   the product ring `Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`** — one for each chart
24	   `i ∈ Fin 2`. The ring map
25	   ```
26	   φ_i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar
27	   ```
28	   sends (chart i = 1, the chart where `zeroPt = [0:1]` lives):
29	   - `X 1 ↦ (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk X 1 isHomogeneous 1) ⊗ 1`
30	     (the canonical lift, a unit in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)` since
31	     `X 1 / X 1 = 1`).
32	   - `X 0 ↦ (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk X 0 isHomogeneous 1) ⊗ λ`
33	     (the affine coord `X 0 / X 1` of `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`, scaled by
34	     `λ ∈ GmRing`).
35	
36	   For chart 0 (symmetric, `X 0` is the unit coord, `X 1 ↦ X 1 / X 0`
37	   scaled by `λ⁻¹` via `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf`).
38	
39	2. **Irrelevant-ideal condition for `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`**: the
40	   image of `(X 0, X 1)` under `φ_i` must generate the unit ideal in
41	   `Γ(Spec (Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing), ⊤)`. Since `X i ↦ (unit lift) ⊗ 1`
42	   in chart `i`, the image of `X i` is a unit, so the irrelevant ideal
43	   maps to the unit ideal. The discharge is exactly the same shape as
44	   `ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top` (L409, axiom-clean) — copy
45	   that template.
46	
47	3. **Glue via `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`** over `projGmCover`
48	   (the pullback of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover` along
49	   `pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar).hom (Gm).hom`, i.e.
50	   `Scheme.Cover.pullback₁`). Cross-chart agreement on `D₊(X 0 · X 1)`:
51	   both chart maps restrict to the same morphism into ℙ¹ on the
52	   intersection. The cleanest discharge: use `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`'s
53	   functoriality + the explicit ring identity
54	   `λ·X 0 ⊗ X 1 = X 0 ⊗ X 1 · λ` (or its chart-form variant).
55	
56	4. **`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body** (L678): chart-level direct
57	   computation. `zeroPt = pointOfVec (v 0 := 0, v 1 := 1) 1` lives in
58	   chart 1; precomposing the chart-1 map with `zeroPt`'s factorization
59	   gives `X 0 ↦ 0 · 1 ⊗ λ = 0` and `X 1 ↦ 1 ⊗ 1 = 1`. The resulting
60	   composite at the `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` level evaluates to
61	   `zeroPt` independent of `λ` — i.e. constant in `Gm`. This is
62	   `toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ zeroPt`. Helper (~15 LOC): a
63	   `zeroPt_factors_through_chart1` lemma derived via
64	   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`
65	   (`ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:493`).
66

71	- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` + `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` +
72	  `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` —
73	  `ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439, 493`.
74	- `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` + `ι_glueMorphisms` + `hom_ext` —
75	  `Gluing.lean:436, 457, 448`.
76	- `Scheme.Cover.pullback₁` + `AffineOpenCover.openCover` —
77	  `Cover/MorphismProperty.lean:177`, `Cover/Open.lean:128`.
78	- `pullbackSpecIso` and its `inv_fst / _snd / hom_fst' / hom_base`

194	  be consumed by the `gmScalingP1` body or the `_collapse_at_zero`
195	  body.** This is the Option B distinguishing constraint. If the
196	  prover finds itself reaching for the iso to close the body, abort
197	  to PARTIAL and document the technical reason.
198	- **`gm_grpObj` MUST NOT be consumed by the `gmScalingP1` body.** The
199	  morphism `σ_× : ℙ¹ × Gm ⟶ ℙ¹` is a `Scheme.Hom`, not a `GrpObj.Hom`;
200	  the group-object structure on the target `A` (consumed by Cor 1.5 in
201	  AVR) is on `A`, not on `Gm`. If the prover finds itself reaching for
202	  `[GrpObj (Gm kbar)]`, the construction is over-specified — find the
203	  bare `Scheme.Hom` route.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects: `ℙ¹`, `Ga`, `Gm`, and the `Gm`-scaling action `σ_×`
10	
11	This file ships the concrete genus-`0` base-case infrastructure (iter-165 lane) that
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes via
13	the **`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut** (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Proposition 3.10): the total
14	scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
15	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`); the `W`-axis collapses at the scaling fixed point
16	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, giving `f(λx) = f(x)`; density of `𝔾_m ⊆ ℙ¹` plus `A` separated
17	(`ext_of_eqOnOpen`) force `f` constant. NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
18	`Hom(𝔾_a, A) = 0`, char-general. See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`
19	(`def:genus0_base_objects`, `def:gaTranslationP1`).
20	
21	The file is **upstream** of `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` (the mathlib-analogist
22	`gm-scaling-p1` D4 verdict: split into a focused file per scheme construction, mirroring
23	the precedent of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.{Smooth,Abelian}`). The AVR.lean refactor +
24	proof of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` is iter-166's lane and does NOT happen here.
25	
26	## The four declarations
27	
28	1. `ProjectiveLineBar` — the projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as `Proj 𝒜` of
29	   the standard ℕ-graded `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, viewed as an object of
30	   `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. `IsProper` is FREE from
31	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` (the algebra is finite type).
32	   `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` are project-side sub-builds
33	   (Mathlib does not ship these for `Proj`), left as scaffold `sorry`s for iter-166+.
34	
35	2. `Ga` — the additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
36	   `(AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver _`. `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
37	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor
38	   `T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; `Smooth` is FREE from
39	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
40	
41	3. `Gm` — the multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄`, encoded as
42	   `(Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))).asOver _`
43	   (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`, AFFINE — NOT the basic-open path). `IsAffine` is FREE; `GrpObj` is
44	   installed via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the units functor
45	   `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`; `Smooth` is FREE from
46	   `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`.
47	
48	4. `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` — the bare scaling
49	   morphism `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, with companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposing
50	   the load-bearing fixed-point property at `0 ∈ ℙ¹` that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`
51	   (Cor 1.5) needs.
52	
53	The blueprint chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` covers this file via
54	`% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
55	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`.
56	-/
57	
58	set_option autoImplicit false
59	set_option linter.style.setOption false
60	
61	universe u
62	
63	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
70	
71	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
72	
73	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
74	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
75	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
76	
77	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
78	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
80	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
81	
82	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
83	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
84	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
85	
86	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
87	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
88	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
89	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
90	
91	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
92	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
93	irreducibility instances. -/
94	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
95	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
96	
97	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
98	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
99	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
100	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
101	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
102	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
103	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
104	
105	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
106	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
107	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
108	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
109	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
110	
111	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
112	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
113	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
114	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
115	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
116	
117	The chain:
118	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
119	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
120	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
121	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
122	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
123	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
124	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
125	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
126	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
127	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
128	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
129	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
130	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
131	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
132	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
133	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
134	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
135	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
136	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
137	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
138	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
139	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
140	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
141	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
142	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
143	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
144	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
145	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
146	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
147	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
148	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
149	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
150	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
151	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
152	    · intro x y h
153	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
154	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
155	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
156	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
157	      apply Subtype.ext
158	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
159	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
160	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
161	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
162	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
163	      exact key
164	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
165	  -- proper.
166	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
167	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
168	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
169	    exact hbij
170	  infer_instance
171	
172	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
173	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
174	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
175	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
176	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
177	  sorry
178	
179	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
180	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
181	acceptable for iter-165). -/
182	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
183	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
184	  sorry
185	
186	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
187	
188	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
189	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
190	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
191	
192	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
193	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
194	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
195	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
196	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
197	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
198	  let f : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
199	  let m : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
200	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (m := m) f
201	    (fun i ↦ by
202	      fin_cases i <;> simp [f, m, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
203	        MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X])
204	    (fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> simp [m])
205	    (by
206	      classical
207	      intro p hp
208	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
209	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
210	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
211	        have := hp
212	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
213	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
214	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
215	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
216	        exact this
217	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
218	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
219	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
220	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
221	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
222	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
223	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
224	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
225	        by_contra h
226	        push Not at h
227	        apply hd_ne
228	        ext k
229	        fin_cases k
230	        · simpa using h.1
231	        · simpa using h.2
232	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
233	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
234	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
235	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
236	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
237	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
238	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
239	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
240	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
241	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
242	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
243	
244	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
245	
246	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
247	private def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
248	  | 0 => 1
249	  | 1 => 0
250	
251	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
252	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
253	
254	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
255	  fin_cases i <;> decide
256	
257	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
258	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
259	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
260	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
261	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
262	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
263	
264	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
265	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
266	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
267	
268	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
269	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
270	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
271	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
272	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
273	
274	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
275	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
276	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
277	
278	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
279	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
280	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
281	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
282	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
283	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
284	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
285	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
286	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
287	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
288	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
289	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
290	
291	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
292	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
293	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
294	    kbar →+*
295	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
296	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
297	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
298	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
299	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
300	
301	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
302	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
303	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
304	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
305	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
306	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
307	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
308	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
309	    (fun _ : Unit =>
310	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
311	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
312	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
313	
314	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
315	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
316	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
317	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
318	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
319	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
320	  · intro r
321	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
322	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
323	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
324	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
325	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
326	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
327	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
328	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
329	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
330	      MvPolynomial.C r
331	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
332	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
333	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
334	  · intro _
335	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
336	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
337	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
338	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
339	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
340	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
341	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
342	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
343	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
344	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
345	      MvPolynomial.X ()
346	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
347	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
348	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
349	
350	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
351	
352	The proof strategy: surjectivity of `inverse` (the image equals `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0)
353	{isLocalizationElem}`, which is `⊤` by `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` applied with
354	`d = 1`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`); together with the already-proved
355	`forward.comp inverse = id`, gives `inverse.comp forward = id` by a "cancel surjective"
356	argument.
357	
358	Full proof of surjectivity is involved (~30 LOC reducing through `Algebra.adjoin` and
359	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top`). Alternative direct approach via
360	`MvPolynomial.IsWeightedHomogeneous.induction_on` on `num` (~50 LOC). Deferred; see
361	analogist `gmscaling-deep.md` Q2 right_inv.
362	
363	**iter-168 partial**: structural setup via `ext`, `Away.mk_surjective`, `val_injective`
364	gets us to the underlying `Localization.Away (X i)` comparison; the residual identity
365	reduces to a monomial-by-monomial check using `Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff` with the
366	cancellation `(X 0)^a · (X 1)^b · (X i)^n = (X 0)^a · (X 1)^b · (X i)^(a + b)` when
367	`a + b = n`. -/
368	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
369	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
370	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
371	      RingHom.id _ := by
372	  sorry
373	
374	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
375	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
376	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
377	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
378	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
379	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
380	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
381	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
382	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
383	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
384	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
385	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
386	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
387	
388	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
389	
390	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
391	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
392	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
393	
394	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
395	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
396	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
397	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
398	
399	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
400	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
401	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
402	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
403	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
404	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
405	
406	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
407	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
408	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
409	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
410	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
411	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
412	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
413	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
414	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
415	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
416	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
417	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
418	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
419	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
420	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
421	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
422	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
423	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
424	    exact hi.map _
425	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
426	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
427	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
428	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
429	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
430	
431	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
432	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
433	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
434	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
435	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
436	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
437	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
438	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
439	  Over.homMk
440	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
441	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
442	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
443	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
444	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
445	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
446	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
447	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
448	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
449	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
450	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
451	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
452	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
453	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
454	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
455	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
456	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
457	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
458	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
459	      ext r
460	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
461	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
462	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
463	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
464	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
465	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
466	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
467	
468	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
469	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
470	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
471	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
472	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
473	
474	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
475	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
476	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
477	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
478	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
479	
480	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
481	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
482	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
483	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
484	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
485	
486	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
487	
488	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
489	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
490	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
491	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
492	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
493	
494	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
495	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
496	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
497	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
498	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
499	
500	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
501	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
502	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
503	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
506	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
507	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
508	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
509	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
510	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
511	
512	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
513	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
514	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
516	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
517	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
518	
519	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
520	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
521	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
522	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
523	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
524	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
525	
526	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_a` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
527	
528	Installs the additive-group structure on `Ga` using the Yoneda installer
529	`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` (Mathlib's canonical mechanism) with the additive-group functor
530	`T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)` and the representable-by witness derived from
531	`AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`. Scaffold: full body is iter-166's lane (the homEquiv +
532	naturality glue is straightforward but needs careful unfolding).
533	
534	Scaffold body for iter-166+; off-path for the genus-0 closure (the rigidity consumer
535	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` uses `W = Gm`, not `W = Ga` — `Ga` is on the demoted
536	additive route only). -/
537	instance ga_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Ga kbar) := sorry
538	
539	/-- **`𝔾_a` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
540	`AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once `GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`,
541	and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
542	instance ga_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
543	    Smooth (Ga kbar).hom :=
544	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Ga kbar).hom) := ga_grpObj kbar
545	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Ga kbar).hom
546	
547	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
548	
549	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
550	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
551	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
552	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
553	
554	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
555	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
556	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
557	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
558	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
559	
560	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
561	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
562	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
563	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
564	
565	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
566	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
567	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
568	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
569	
570	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
571	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
572	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
573	
574	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
575	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
576	element is finitely presented), bridged through
577	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
578	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
579	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
580	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
581	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
582	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
583	      inferInstance)
584	
585	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
586	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
587	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
588	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
589	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
590	
591	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
592	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
593	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
594	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
595	
596	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
597	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
598	  unfold GmRing
599	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
600	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
601	
602	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
603	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
604	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
605	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
606	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
607	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
608	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
609	  infer_instance
610	
611	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
612	
613	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
614	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
615	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
616	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection). Scaffold body — same
617	discipline as `ga_grpObj`.
618	
619	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
620	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
621	`W = Gm`). -/
622	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := sorry
623	
624	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
625	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
626	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
627	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
628	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
629	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
630	
631	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
632	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
633	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
634	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
635	  η[Gm kbar]
636	
637	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
638	
639	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
640	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
641	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
642	
643	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
644	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
645	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
646	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
647	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
648	
649	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
650	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
651	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
652	
653	/-- **The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`** in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.
654	
655	Scaffold body — iter-165 lands the *type signature* (the concrete object the iter-166
656	proof refactor of `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` consumes); the chartwise glue body

683	(in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), so its previously local `haveI ... := by sorry`

709	for a domain graded ring; would close via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover`
710	once `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is bridged to `IsDomain` for the standard ℕ-grading
711	on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`). Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`).

713	The strategy:
714	1. `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `Proj.affineOpenCover (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)`.
715	2. Each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))` reduces to showing the ring is a
716	   domain.

718	   isomorphic to `k̄[X_{1-i}/X_i]` (polynomial ring in one variable over a field, a domain). -/
719	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :

721	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
722	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
723	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
724	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
725	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
726	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
727	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
728	    intro i
729	    -- (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i = Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))
730	    -- Need IsReduced of that Spec.
731	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
732	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
733	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
734	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
735	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
736	      fin_cases i <;>
737	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
738	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
739	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
740	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
741	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
742	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
743	        (algebraMap
744	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
745	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
746	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
747	          (Localization.Away
748	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
749	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
750	      intro x y h
751	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
752	    infer_instance
753	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover

755	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Scaffold (Mathlib gap: the

757	closed)` is not bridged; the analogist's recipe would require base-change reduction via

1140	  % a field is reduced) via the explicit chart cover of \cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1141	  % and the canonical $\mathrm{val}$-injection of a homogeneous localization into the
1142	  % corresponding plain localization. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1143	  The scheme underlying $\overline{\mathbb P^1}$ is reduced. By the two-chart affine cover
1144	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1145	  reduced. The chart-$i$ ring $\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i$ embeds into the ordinary
1146	  localization $\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$ of the polynomial ring $\bar k[X_0, X_1]$ at
1147	  the powers of $X_i$ via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1148	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1149	  ($\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}$); the codomain is a localization of a
1150	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of $X_i$), hence a domain, hence
1151	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1152	  ($\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}$ on the $\mathrm{val}$-embedding), so each chart
1153	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1154	\end{lemma}

1156	\begin{definition}
1157	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ (primary), and the $\mathbb G_a$-translation companion $\sigma$]
1158	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
1159	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
1160	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
1161	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
1162	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- $\mathbb G_m$ acts on
1163	  % $\mathbb P^1$ by scalings fixing $0$ and $\infty$, $\mathbb G_a$ by translations fixing $\infty$
1164	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of $\mathrm{PGL}_2$ acting on $\mathbb P^1$).
1165	  % NOTE (iter-164/167): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the
1166	  % MULTIPLICATIVE scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\lean name gmScalingP1, landed iter-165/166;
1167	  % pinned in this block's \lean{...} hook). The $\mathbb G_a$-translation $\sigma$ (\lean name
1168	  % gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive alternative and is
1169	  % intentionally not yet a Lean target.
1170	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
1171	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1172	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
1173	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1}) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
1174	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
1175	  fixing both $0$ and $\infty$) --- defined chartwise:
1176	  \begin{itemize}
1177	    \item On $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (target chart $x$): $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$,
1178	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
1179	    \item Near $\infty$ (target chart $u = 1/x$): the target coordinate $1/(\lambda x)$ rewrites as
1180	      $u/\lambda$, regular everywhere on this chart because $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$ is invertible.
1181	  \end{itemize}
1182	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so $\sigma_\times$ is defined on
1183	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group $\mathbb G_m$ on $\mathbb P^1$:
1184	  $\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x$ and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
1185	  \lambda\mu)$. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
1186	  $\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty$ for all $\lambda$; the fixed point $0$ is what makes the
1187	  $W$-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
1188	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
1189	
1190	  \medskip
1191	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the $\mathbb G_a$-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
1192	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
1193	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1194	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
1195	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
1196	  of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a$, defined chartwise: on $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a$
1197	  (chart $x$) it is the polynomial map $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$; near $\infty$ ($u = 1/x$) the
1198	  coordinate $1/(x + y)$ rewrites as $u/(1 + u y)$, regular at $u = 0$ (value $0$ there --- $\sigma$
1199	  fixes $\infty$ for every $y$), while the locus $x + y = 0$ is covered by the chart $x$ on which it
1200	  is the finite value $0$. It is a left action of $\mathbb G_a$ on $\mathbb P^1$ fixing $\infty$:
1201	  $\sigma(x, 0) = x$ and $\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')$. This companion underlies
1202	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
1203	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
1204	  genus-$0$ critical path.
1205	\end{definition}
1206	
1207	\begin{lemma}
1208	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action fixes the origin $0 \in \mathbb P^1$]
1209	  \label{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}
1210	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero}
1211	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, def:p1bar_zero, def:gm_one}
1212	  % Archon-original; the fact is elementary (σ_×(0, λ) = λ · 0 = 0). No external % SOURCE QUOTE
1213	  % needed for the Lean encoding.
1214	  Let $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ be the scaling action
1215	  of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Then the composite
1216	  \[ \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1},\ \mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m})\;}
1217	     \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_\times\;} \mathbb P^1 \]
1218	  equals the constant morphism $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1}$. That is,
1219	  $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ for every $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$.
1220	\end{lemma}
1221	
1222	\begin{proof}
1223	  \uses{def:gaTranslationP1, def:p1bar_zero, def:gm_one}
1224	  Chart-level. On the affine chart $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (coordinate $x$), the
1225	  morphism $\sigma_\times$ is the polynomial map $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda \cdot x$. Restricting
1226	  to the $\mathbb G_m$-section $x = 0$ gives the constant ring map
1227	  $\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \to \bar k$, $\lambda \mapsto 0$; equivalently the scheme morphism factors
1228	  through the closed point $0 \in \mathbb P^1$. The agreement with the other chart $u = 1/x$ near
1229	  $\infty$ is automatic (the section $x = 0$ does not meet that chart). The named equation is the
1230	  Lean encoding of this fact, with $\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m}$ supplying the
1231	  $\Spec \bar k$-factor and $\mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m}$ the parameter $\lambda$.
1232	\end{proof}
